channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: 21 horas en Pakistán: el intento y fracaso de la paz entre EE. UU. e Irán
item.description: Aliados y adversarios de Estados Unidos habían puesto sus esperanzas en el vicepresidente JD Vance para intentar encontrar una salida a un conflicto que ha trastornado la economía mundial.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Foto de consorcio por Jacquelyn Martin
media:content.media:description: Vance, a la izquierda, reunido con el primer ministro de Pakistán, Shehbaz Sharif, en Islamabad el sábado.

item.title: Can Brooklyn’s Grand Army Plaza Be Car-Free? Mamdani Says Yes.
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is expected to propose linking Grand Army Plaza with Prospect Park by closing a dangerous stretch of road between them.
item.dc:creator: Stefanos Chen

media:content.media:description: Residents and civic groups have complained about traffic whizzing by Grand Army Plaza for decades.

item.title: A Famed Brooklyn Cemetery Wants You to Come Visit … Before You Die
item.description: Green-Wood Cemetery in Brooklyn is opening a $43 million visitor’s center to attract the living by making it easier to navigate the rambling grounds.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Hu

media:content.media:description: Green-Wood Cemetery’s new visitor’s center has been built around a restored Victorian greenhouse.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 13, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: La guerra de Trump está debilitando a EE. UU. de 4 maneras
item.description: El presidente debería reconocer la ineptitud de su enfoque impulsivo y egoísta.
item.dc:creator: El Comité Editorial
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Jason Hendardy para The New York Times

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Oil Climbs Above $100 as U.S. Plans Hormuz Blockade
item.description: The U.S. said it would block ships entering or exiting Iranian ports or coastal areas starting at 10 a.m. Eastern on Monday. It was unclear how that would affect the cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:description: A vessel in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Oman’s Musandam province on Sunday.

item.title: Word of the Day: irrevocable
item.description: This word has appeared in 37 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Mamdani Announces City-Owned Grocery Store
item.description: At a rally on Sunday marking his first 100 days as the mayor of New York City, Zohran Mamdani announced that it would open a city-owned grocery store in East Harlem.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yi

media:content.media:description: La Marqueta, a marketplace under elevated train tracks, once covered five city blocks and had many vendors, but now hosts just a few.

item.title: After Chaotic Election Day, Peru Orders 2nd Day of Voting for Some Areas
item.description: Frustrated voters in long lines were unable to cast ballots in parts of the capital, Lima, due to logistical failures, eroding trust for some in the process.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Glatsky and Mitra Taj

media:content.media:credit: Sara Wayra for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A presidential campaign event for Rafael López Aliaga, leader of the Popular Renewal Party, in Lima last Thursday.

item.title: In the Desert, a ‘Cleaning Station’ for Ants
item.description: A unique “choreography” between two ant species suggests a distinctive partnership in which one provides a carwash service to the other.
item.dc:creator: Alexa Robles-Gil

item.title: The Relationship Between America and the U.A.E. Needs an Upgrade
item.description: For decades, the U.S.-Gulf relationship was based on oil for security. That framework is obsolete.
item.dc:creator: Badr Jafar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: ‘Margo’s Got Money Troubles,’ Plus 5 Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: This adaptation of a novel comes to Apple TV, and the second season of “Beef” premieres on Netflix.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:description: Elle Fanning plays a young mother in “Margo’s Got Money Troubles.”

item.title: In Myanmar, Even Flowers Stoke Fear
item.description: Myanmar’s generals have brutalized the country for decades. But they have come to dread garlands as a sign of resistance.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech and Daniel Berehulak

media:content.media:description: The bombs come at any time. The only constant is that they will.

item.title: ‘The Strangest Sleepover Ever’: Dutch Skeptical as Their Royals Visit Trump
item.description: The Dutch king and queen are set to stay at the White House on Monday night. At a time of trans-Atlantic tension, the Netherlands isn’t sure how it feels about that.
item.dc:creator: Koba Ryckewaert and Jeanna Smialek
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: King Willem-Alexander and Queen Máxima of the Netherlands are headed to Washington this week to meet with President Trump.

item.title: Quote of the Day: However Real the Issues, Congress More Resembles ‘The Real Housewives’
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, April 13, 2025

item.title: What to Know About Pope Leo’s Voyage to Africa
item.description: The pontiff will visit four countries on the world’s poorest continent, demonstrating his urgent focus on the most vulnerable members of the Church.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich

media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican last month. His 10-day trip to Africa begins on Monday and includes visits to Algeria, Angola, Cameroon and Equatorial Guinea.

item.title: Pope Leo Heads to Africa to Meet the Future of Catholicism
item.description: On a four-country tour of the continent, where the faith is growing faster than anywhere else, the pontiff will be greeted by enthusiastic crowds and authoritarian leaders.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV in November in Istanbul. On that visit, the pope engaged in interfaith dialogue, and similar discussions may come up during his Africa trip.

item.title: China’s Electrostate Is Poised to Win From War in the Middle East
item.description: A cluster of Chinese firms is poised to win big from the energy crisis set off by the war in the Middle East, building on booming investment in artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin and Keith Bradsher

media:content.media:credit: Gilles Sabrié for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An electricity transmission hub is at the terminus in south-central China of the world’s longest and most powerful ultrahigh-voltage power line. Chinese companies dominate the manufacturing of nearly every piece of a modern grid.

item.title: Aiming at China, Malaysia Puts New Restrictions on Electric Cars
item.description: The move was a tacit acknowledgment of China’s dominance in the global market and the power of its automakers to keep prices low.
item.dc:creator: Zunaira Saieed

media:content.media:description: An electric car made by Perodua, a Malaysian automaker, at a launch event in Kuala Lumpur in December.

item.title: Orban Loss May Ease Hungary’s Tensions with European Union
item.description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban has stymied the European Union for years, and particularly in recent months. Peter Magyar’s election could change that, if only so much.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek

media:content.media:description: Celebrations in Budapest on Sunday after election results started coming in.

item.title: No Corrections: April 13, 2025
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Monday, April 13, 2025.

item.title: Trump Attacks Pope Leo as Too Liberal and ‘Weak on Crime’
item.description: President Trump’s lengthy post on social media showed there were really no boundaries when it comes to people he might target, including the leader of the world’s 1.4 billion Catholics.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump speaking to the news media at Joint Base Andrews on Sunday night.

item.title: ‘The Comeback’ Season 3, Episode 4: The Blow-Up
item.description: Valerie is losing her patience and self-control. Who can blame her?
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray

media:content.media:description: Valerie (Lisa Kudrow) is giving her all to A.I.-written slop while Billy (Dan Bucatinsky) is accepting industry honors for doing nothing. She should probably be even angrier than she is.

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: ‘Titaníque’ on Broadway: Wild ‘Titanic’ Parody, With Celine Dion as Kooky Guide
item.description: This “Titanic” parody fueled by Dion’s hits, silly ad-libs and pop culture references had the humblest of beginnings. Now the show has docked on Broadway.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli

item.title: Mamdani Returns to the Stage to Tell the Story of His First 100 Days
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani delivered his 100-day address at a concert venue in Queens. He highlighted his accomplishments and was joined by a not-so-surprising guest.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rubinstein and Olivia Bensimon

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “With what we’ve accomplished in 14 weeks, imagine what we can do together in four years,” Mayor Zohran Mamdani said.

item.title: El presidente de Cuba se muestra desafiante en medio de tensiones con EE. UU.
item.description: En “Meet the Press”, el presidente cubano reafirma su postura mientras el foco de Washington sigue puesto en Irán.
item.dc:creator: Adam B. Ellick and Simon Romero

media:content.media:description: El presidente de Cuba, Miguel Díaz-Canel, en La Habana el mes pasado. El domingo dijo a Meet the Press: “Nos defenderemos, y si nos toca morir, moriremos”.

item.title: Trump Says Gas Prices Might Not Drop By Midterms, Highlighting G.O.P. Peril
item.description: The president has sent mixed messages on how long he expects costs to be high. Some in his party voiced fresh concern after his comments on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Nathan Howard for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump told Fox News that gas prices might be “a little higher” by the November midterms.

item.title: How Would a Blockade of the Strait of Hormuz Work? Here Are Some Possibilities.
item.description: The goal of the naval operation ordered by President Trump is to deny Iran the revenues it needs to fund its military, but there are many unknowns.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump said the U.S. would blockade the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Eric Swalwell Suspends Campaign for California Governor After Sexual Assault Allegations
item.description: In a social media post, the representative said he was “deeply sorry for mistakes in judgment I’ve made in my past,” but promised to fight what he called “false allegations.”
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan

media:content.media:description: Representative Eric Swalwell of California had been in a crowded field vying to succeed Gov. Gavin Newsom.

item.title: Estampida en fortaleza de Haití deja al menos 30 muertos
item.description: La tragedia tuvo lugar en la Citadelle Laferrière, fortaleza histórica del norte de Haití y uno de los símbolos más característicos del país.
item.dc:creator: André Paultre, Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and Simon Romero

media:content.media:description: La Citadelle Laferrière, una gran fortaleza del siglo XIX en el norte de Haití, en 2024.

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: Artemis II: astronautas volvieron de la Luna y la NASA cobró impulso
item.description: Los astronautas rompieron varios récords y pudieron estudiar partes de la cara oculta de la Luna.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang

media:content.media:credit: Bill Ingalls/NASA, vía Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Al caer en el océano Pacífico el viernes cerca de San Diego, la misión Artemis II llegó a una conclusión exitosa.

item.title: Elon Musk, Who Owns X, Appears to Post on TikTok
item.description: A verified account with the @elonmusk handle also recently showed up on Instagram, as the billionaire prepares to take his rocket company SpaceX public.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac

media:content.media:description: Elon Musk has previously shown disdain for other social media companies that X competes against.

item.title: Former Staffers Call on Eric Swalwell to Quit Campaign and Resign from House
item.description: In a signed letter, they called on law enforcement officials to “open a full and thorough investigation” of the California representative.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales

media:content.media:description: Eric Swalwell at the U.S. Capitol in January.

item.title: Hungary’s Orban Concedes Election Defeat
item.description: Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orban, who has inspired populist movements globally, conceded defeat on Sunday in a general election.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban on Sunday. His defeat will please the European Union and is likely to disappoint President Trump.

item.title: Scores Killed in Nigerian Military Strikes as Clashes With Militants Intensify
item.description: Nigerian officials said they attacked a terrorist enclave, but locals and rights groups said the airstrikes hit a popular market, killing many civilians.
item.dc:creator: Saikou Jammeh and Eric Schmitt

item.title: Oil Prices Surge Above $100 After Peace Talks Fail and Trump Threatens Blockade
item.description: Oil prices surged as markets reopened following news that U.S. peace talks with Iran had ended without a deal.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: An Underwater Phantom Is Killing Fish and Breaking Hearts
item.description: A mysterious algal bloom has haunted South Australia’s coast for more than a year, killing marine life and unsettling communities that cherish the ocean.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 13, 2026
item.description: Brad Wiegmann gives us a puzzle worth celebrating.
item.dc:creator: Peter Blair

media:content.media:description: With these hints, you’ll wrap up this puzzle in no time.

item.title: Christine Fréchette Is Chosen to Lead Coalition Avenir Québec
item.description: Far behind in the polls, Quebec’s governing party chose Christine Fréchette to succeed François Legault and to lead it in a vote this fall.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi

media:content.media:description: Christine Fréchette, in Drummondville, Quebec, on Sunday.

item.title: How Lee Zeldin Shifted the Mission — And the Message — of the E.P.A.
item.description: More than any administrator in decades, Lee Zeldin talks about the Environmental Protection Agency's work in economic terms, reflecting President Trump’s desire to boost industry while downplaying environmental consequences.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Friedman and Harry Stevens

item.title: How Al Sharpton Sized Up the Potential 2028 Democrats at His Convention
item.description: In an interview with The New York Times, the Rev. Al Sharpton reflected on the performances of 10 Democrats who spoke and explained why two did not.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:description: The Rev. Al Sharpton interviewed more than a half-dozen Democrats seen as potential presidential contenders.

item.title: Israel’s War
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was instrumental in taking the U.S. to war with Iran. Now, he is trying to prolong the fighting.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Shaping the New Look of ‘Euphoria’
item.description: Natasha Newman-Thomas has stepped in as the new costume designer for the third season of HBO’s chaotic megahit. It’s a lofty task.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: NASA’s Artemis II Crew Returns to Houston After Lunar Mission
item.description: After splashing down in the Pacific Ocean, the Artemis II crew members reunited with their friends, families and fellow NASA astronauts in Houston on Saturday. Their voyage was the first trip by humans into deep space in more than half a century.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:description: In Houston.

item.title: How 6 People in New York City Are Coping With Higher Costs
item.description: Surging inflation and rising prices have some people re-evaluating date nights and how much of their splurges they can share with friends.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy and Vincent Alban
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times; Claire Fahy/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: People in New York City said they were cutting back in different ways as higher costs for essentials and fun increase at the same time.

item.title: Mamdani Plans to Open City-Owned Grocery Store in East Harlem
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced plans for a city-owned grocery store at La Marqueta, moving to deliver on a campaign pledge.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons

media:content.media:description: La Marqueta, a marketplace under elevated train tracks, once covered five city blocks and had many vendors, but now hosts just a few.

item.title: Iran’s Underground Air Force Base Was Hit Earlier in the War
item.description: Satellite imagery shows impact craters at and near the tunnel entrances to aircraft shelters at Iran’s remote “Eagle 44” base.
item.dc:creator: Christoph Koettl
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: One Dead and 6 Injured in Shooting at Suburban Chick-fil-A
item.description: There have been no arrests in the Saturday night shooting in New Jersey. The authorities said it was not a random attack.
item.dc:creator: Jay Root and Davaughnia Wilson

media:content.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The shooting took place around 8:45 p.m. on Saturday in a Chick-fil-A restaurant about a 10-minute drive from Newark.
